case_name: ABC  v Basildon & Thurrock Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust [2020] EWHC 3903 (QB) (27 April 2020)

summary.brief: Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The Claimant is 20 years old, but because he lacks capacity these proceedings have been brought on his behalf by his mother as litigation friend.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He was born on 8 th April 2000 at the very young gestational age of 27 weeks at the Basildon Hospital in Essex.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He suffered severe injury in the form of intracranial haemorrhage in the germinal matrix area of his brain.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: This has given rise to asymmetric spastic cerebral palsy.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The case advanced on behalf of the Claimant was that his mother should have been given antenatal corticosteroid treatment in the days before the birth.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The defendant accepted that the failure to give the mother corticosteroids was negligent, but said that these would not have made any difference.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Claimant went to a local mainstream primary school, secondary school and sixth form college.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He has a number of GCSEs and has undertaken two work placements.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He does however have impaired motor control in his right arm and hand and in both legs.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The dexterity in his right hand has improved to some extent, but his lower limb function has not.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He trips and falls frequently and uses a stick.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He is likely to have to use a wheelchair outside and possibly inside too in later life.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He also suffers from anxiety, and his development is a little behind that of some of his peers.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The reasonableness of the settlement must be approached with that firmly in mind.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: I am satisfied, on the basis of his analysis of those issues, that it is entirely justified to accept a settlement that reflects litigation risk in the way he has outlined.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: I am satisfied that these figures are appropriate ones which it is the Claimant's best interests to accept.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

patient_demographics.relevant_social_history: Lives with mother and two sisters, close family unit

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Born at 27 weeks gestation, suffered intracranial haemorrhage leading to cerebral palsy

focused_exam.neurological: Impaired motor control in right arm and hand, both legs; poor balance; uses a stick

requestables.name: Neurology Consultation
requestables.description: Assessment of motor function and cerebral palsy management

reveal.result_summary: Motor impairment consistent with cerebral palsy
reveal.decision_impact: Supports need for ongoing physiotherapy and potential future wheelchair use

timeline_phases.name: Antenatal Care
timeline_phases.description: Mother's care prior to preterm delivery

decision_points.clinical_context: Mother at risk of preterm delivery

options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care to improve neonatal outcomes in preterm birth

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence supports improved outcomes with corticosteroids

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to administer corticosteroids was negligent
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Corticosteroids should have been given to improve lung function

patient_outcome.description: Patient has asymmetric spastic cerebral palsy with motor impairments
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Impaired motor control, likely future wheelchair use

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to administer corticosteroids

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Corticosteroids might have improved neonatal outcomes
